data.title: Musk Addresses X Platform Outage and Operational Challenges
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Elon Musk responded to news about the 'X Platform outage' on social media, stating that he has returned to a 24/7 work schedule, often sleeping in meeting rooms, server rooms, or factory spaces. Musk emphasized the need for intense focus on X/xAI and Tesla, especially with the upcoming Starship launch next week, as they are rolling out critical technologies.

Musk highlighted that the recent outage of the X platform demonstrated the necessity for significant operational improvements. He noted that failover redundancy should have been effective but did not work as intended.

data.title: X Platform Faces Major Network Disruption Due to Data Center Fire
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the X platform experienced a significant network disruption, potentially linked to a fire at a data center in Oregon. Starting from 8:42 PM UTC+8, over 25,000 users reported unusual network issues on the platform. The cause of the disruption is under investigation, with the fire at the data center being a possible factor.

data.title: Cryptocurrency Market Experiences $266 Million Liquidation in 24 Hours
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from Coinglass reveals that the cryptocurrency market witnessed liquidations totaling $266 million over the past 24 hours. Of this amount, long positions accounted for $204 million, while short positions saw liquidations of $61.54 million.

data.title: BlackRock Adds $877 Million in BTC as Momentum Builds Toward $120K
data.description:
Bitcoin’s rise continues as BlackRock strengthens its position in the digital asset market. The financial firm has purchased 8,000 BTC through its BlackRock IBIT fund, marking its largest crypto investment in May. The purchase, valued at $877. 2 million, brings BlackRock’s total Bitcoin holdings to over 639,000 BTC
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/05/24/blackrock-adds-877-million-in-btc-as-momentum-builds-toward-120k/

source: https://www.theblock.co/post/355653/crypto-investor-charged-in-sadistic-kidnap-torture-of-italian-tourist-in-nyc-reports?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss

data.title: An American crypto investor is suspected of kidnapping and torturing an Italian businessman in New York in an attempt to obtain his financial account passwords
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: John Woeltz, a 37-year-old American cryptocurrency investor, and a 24-year-old suspected female accomplice were accused of illegally detaining and torturing a 28-year-old Italian businessman in a high-end apartment in Manhattan, New York, for more than two weeks in an attempt to obtain the password of his financial account. The victim arrived in New York on May 6 and met with John Woeltz, with whom he had business dealings. He was then deprived of his passport and subjected to electric shocks, drug coercion, chainsaw threats and other abuses.
It is reported that the police found several Polaroid photos of the victim being tied up and tortured at the scene, as well as guns, chainsaws and other tools. The victim finally escaped and called the police on May 23. John Woeltz was charged with multiple crimes including first-degree kidnapping, illegal detention, assault and illegal possession of weapons. (The Block)

data.title: Phishing Attack Results in Significant Financial Loss
data.description: According to PANews, a phishing attack has led to a substantial financial loss of $146,551 due to malicious bulk transactions. The victim's address involved in the incident is 0xc6d289d55fe64227a09e3120855ccba0d2e606dc. The attackers' addresses are identified as 0xC83De81A2aa92640D8d68ddf3Fc6b4B853D77359 and 0x33dAD2bbb03Dca73a3d92FC2413A1F8D09c34181.

data.title: Musk: I have to pay close attention to X/xAI and Tesla
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Musk posted on the X platform: "I have returned to working 24 hours a day and sleeping in conference rooms/servers/factory rooms. I must pay close attention to X/xAI and Tesla because we have key technologies being launched. This week's X uptime issues show that major operational improvements are needed. Failover redundancy should have worked, but it didn't."
